mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with declining unemployment signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 4.0% fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving manufacturing activity that enhances corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO changes (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts indicate weak consumer demand that pressures corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with HOUST declines (Rank IC > -0.05) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% erodes real interest rates, prompting central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL > 3.5% in 60%+ of cases, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03 in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal deteriorating demand conditions that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment claims (UNRATE) during a recessionary phase signals deteriorating labor market conditions that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive despite UNRATE > 4.5% in 60%+ of cases, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03 in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during a supply-side shock indicates overcapacity that pressures profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO > 105 (Rank IC > -0.05) over any 1-year rolling window.
